examples.code:
conn = sqlite3.connect(':memory:')
conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row  # Access columns by name!
cur = conn.cursor()
cur.execute('SELECT 1 as id, "Alice" as name')
row = cur.fetchone()
print(row['id'], row['name']) # 1 Alice
examples.explanation: sqlite3.Row converts query results into dict-like accessible row objects.
